entries.mem0ai/mem0: A universal memory layer providing multi-level state for users, sessions, and agents. It offers hybrid retrieval via semantic, keyword, and entity matching with flexible cloud or self-hosted deployment options.
entries.volcengine/OpenViking: An open-source context database utilizing a filesystem-based organizational paradigm and tiered L0/L1/L2 loading. It significantly reduces token usage and improves task completion through recursive directory retrieval.
entries.supermemoryai/supermemory: A high-performance memory engine featuring a custom vector graph and byte-level deduplication for 100% prompt-cache discounts. It maintains persistent memory graphs and automated user profiling across multiple conversations.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agents that automatically extracts typed entity relationships into a self-wiring knowledge graph. Its synthesis layer provides cited prose answers and explicit gap analysis for multi-user company environments.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-focused memory system implementing retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent growth. It provides a simple LLM wrapper compatible with OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and local models.
entries.campfirein/byterover-cli: A memory provider that treats context as a git repository, allowing for branching, merging, and versioning of markdown context trees. It features a sub-100ms 5-tier retrieval system and MCP integration.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A stateful agent library that extracts reasoning-based conclusions from conversations rather than simple text chunks. It models multi-peer perspectives to track knowledge between different entities.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive self-improving harness that distills successful agent strategies into reusable playbooks. It employs multi-role coordination for strategy proposal and curation to solve complex tasks.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system using a SQLite backend to achieve sub-millisecond retrieval performance. It manages long-term knowledge through episodic compression and temporal triples.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin utilizing a hierarchical summary DAG to prevent message loss during compaction. It includes specialized tools like lcm_grep for raw message recovery and sensitive-pattern redaction.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for Honcho that enables private cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM or local inference server via Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis.
entries.Signet-AI/signetai: A local-first identity and secrets layer that provides portable state across Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and MCP clients. It features a repairable memory system for editing and reclassifying agent context.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: An on-device context engine that operates entirely locally without cloud dependencies or API keys. It combines BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs for hybrid retrieval.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer using an open YAML engram format for multi-agent systems. It employs ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts while decaying stale information over time.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that increases cache hits and reduces prefill latency through prefix reordering and deduplication. It accelerates inference across SGLang, vLLM, and llama.cpp.
entries.yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin: A self-maintaining plugin providing explainable retrieval via 'why_retrieved' tags. It tracks contradictions and manages agent-authored skills and outcome ledgers.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: A memory kernel stack that consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval. It leverages SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma to provide bounded evidence packing and reduce prompt noise.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system combining BM25 full-text search with ranked graph traversal. It automatically indexes local directories, URLs, and multimedia files using navigable edge tags.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It uses local-first storage with SQLite-vec and GGUF models via an MCP-native server.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: A utility that reduces prompt token overhead by using local BM25 ranking to select only necessary tool schemas. It includes a dashboard to track estimated token savings.
entries.Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin: A local-only plugin using a columnar .lbdb embedded graph database with importance-weighted recall. It surfaces high-priority memories first and supports optional GLiNER2 entity extraction.
entries.MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory: A pull-model episodic memory plugin that ensures complete deletion of memories without leaving persistent summaries. It provides a trace.jsonl audit log for every memory operation.
entries.futurebrowser/hermes-exabase-plugin: An integration with Exabase M-1 for self-organizing long-term agent memory. It provides tools for manual storage, query expansion, and result reranking.

entries.nexu-io/open-design: A local-first design system that enables Hermes Agent to generate HTML prototypes, MP4 motion graphics, and PPTX decks. It bridges the gap between design and code by integrating 150 design systems and 261 plugins via a coding-agent CLI.
entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: A comprehensive library of 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It utilizes the agentskills.io standard to enable sub-second skill discovery across 26 security domains.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes Agent to generate draw.io diagrams from natural language, supporting presets for ERDs, UML, and Architecture. It provides automated structure diagrams for multiple languages and access to over 10,000 official shapes.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: A content auditing tool that eliminates detectable AI writing patterns through rewrite, detect, and edit modes. It employs a two-pass detection system to ensure human-like output and is compatible with Hermes and other leading coding agents.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: An agentic evolver that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It unifies skill libraries across different agents and teams with native integration for Hermes and OpenAI-compatible APIs.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library providing modular frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy. These skills can be installed via a marketplace or the npx skills CLI for use with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose reusable skills. It streamlines development by generating both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds via dedicated slash commands.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project brings type-safe agentskills.io support to Pydantic AI using progressive disclosure to reduce token usage. It allows for flexible skill definitions through either programmatic Python or the filesystem.
entries.ZeroPointRepo/youtube-skills: A suite of tools for extracting YouTube transcripts, captions, and timestamps without relying on yt-dlp. It enables Hermes Agent to perform video searches, browse channels, and extract playlists efficiently.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and installing skills featuring automated security scanning with 65+ threat detection rules. It includes a creator marketplace with crypto/Stripe payments and an agent-to-agent trust scoring system.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: These skills teach Hermes Agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problems. It helps agents identify structural conservation laws in codebases and generate detailed constraint reports on analysis blind spots.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: A harness for long-running agents that implements structured memory and a plan-approve-execute lifecycle. It ensures reliability through verification gates requiring recorded proof and stores all state as local plain files.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: A literate programming skill that weaves code and documentation into .lit.md files. It features a tangler to extract runnable source files and automatically syncs edits between the source code and the literate document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: A memory and model replacement system that uses markdown-based fabric directories for cross-instance shared memory. It provides an end-to-end pipeline for extracting training data and fine-tuning replacement models.
entries.AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow: A pre-build workflow that transforms rough ideas into structured design documents and implementation handoffs. It offers Lite and Full modes and includes a spec review gate with PASS/FAIL evaluation models.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Specialized skills for interacting with the Chainlink oracle network, supporting CCIP cross-chain messaging and VRF v2.5 management. It provides the necessary tools for integrating Data Feeds and Data Streams via the agentskills.io spec.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: A conversion tool that transforms repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant skills. It supports version-pinned, immutable skills and links instructions directly to specific file lines or URLs.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official FLUX image generation skills providing standardized prompting guidelines for the FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 families. It includes API integration patterns for webhooks and polling, supporting precise hex color specifications and JSON prompting.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A registry for reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification, featuring an OpenAPI-driven Python client. It supports versioned uploads with recursive dependency resolution and integrated payment rails via TON and Stripe.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: A task execution skill designed for complex, multi-step processes with real-time progress tracking. It ensures stability for long-running tasks through built-in checkpoints and rollback recovery procedures.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that utilizes three-layer lazy loading to minimize token consumption. It manages persistent MCP servers via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading of JSON configurations.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: A skill that enables Hermes Agent to manage Nextcloud files, notes, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV. It ensures secure communication over HTTPS using Nextcloud App Passwords and includes a guided setup script.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: A C# / .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with MCP integration. It leverages Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based loading and implements progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files.
entries.Sahil-SS9/hermes-multichannel-prompt-opt: A plugin that rewrites and scores prompts for various surfaces, including Discord, Telegram, and CLI. It reduces token usage by 20–80% by removing noise and tailoring structures to specific model reasoning capabilities.
entries.willingning-coder/eagle-eye: An intelligent retrieval system using a 5-layer approach—including FTS5, dense embeddings, and RRF fusion—to filter 100+ skills down to the five most relevant. This zero-invasive integration significantly reduces token consumption.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a collection of Nix packages for various AI coding agents and CLIs, including Hermes. It features automated daily updates to ensure users always have access to the most recent tool versions within a reproducible Nix environment.
entries.marshallrichards/ClawPhone: ClawPhone offers specialized scripts and configuration guides for running agent CLIs on ARM64 Android devices via Termux. It integrates with Termux:API and Termux:GUI, allowing Hermes and other OpenClaw agents to interact directly with smartphone hardware.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: This zero-install Windows desktop solution provides a GUI-driven Hermes experience with 100 built-in tools and local model support via LM Studio. It functions as a portable automation engine, allowing users to build custom workflow pipelines without requiring Docker or complex installations.
entries.AbuZar-Ansarii/Hermes-Agent-On-Android: Designed for mobile privacy and cost-efficiency, this project features a one-line Termux installer for running Hermes Agent locally on Android. It supports over 200 models, including local execution via Ollama and remote access through major API providers.
entries.ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart: This community Helm chart enables cloud-native deployment of Hermes Agent on Kubernetes with a focus on state safety. It enforces single-writer storage patterns and offers configurable gateway modes, making it suitable for operator-ready production workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw provides a high-security sandbox for Hermes Agent using kernel-level enforcement to restrict network, filesystem, and process access. It allows users to apply dynamic security policies while integrating the agent with IDEs, MCP servers, and messaging platforms.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent in a sandboxed environment. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific versions of Hermes using build arguments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes deployments, including systemd service configuration and secure secrets handling via sops-nix. It bridges the gap between Nix-managed reproducibility and interactive CLI usage for a hybrid deployment model.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Focused on production-ready headless operation, this tool configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is specifically designed for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integration with the Nous Portal.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It features workspace isolation, task scheduling, and inter-agent delegation, providing an enterprise-grade management layer for complex agent hierarchies.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option provides a Docker Compose stack optimized for Portainer, featuring a ttyd web terminal for remote browser-based access. It simplifies management through a Git Repository stack and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration stack provides a zero-dependency SQLite environment for dispatching tasks across CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen workflows. It distinguishes itself with the Aegis review system, which implements quality gates to monitor agent spend and task accuracy.
entries.agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires: A versatile control surface that manages parallel sessions for Hermes, Claude Code, and other agents using tmux for persistent background execution. It offers a mobile-first web dashboard and TUI, utilizing Docker or Podman to ensure all agent activities remain sandboxed.
entries.CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room: This template introduces a sidecar control plane focused on agent governance and documentation for scaling from single VPS instances to specialist teams. It utilizes a structured task bus pattern to facilitate formal delegation between orchestrators and sub-agents.
entries.Agent-3-7/minions: Minions provides a centralized mission control interface featuring a Kanban board for tracking autonomous task statuses and review queues. It emphasizes human-in-the-loop verification by live-streaming agent reasoning and tool execution for real-time supervision.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous swarms across 23+ providers, including Ollama and Hermes, with built-in heartbeats and scheduling. It features a unique 'conversation-to-skill' learning mechanism and durable memory to evolve agent capabilities over time.
entries.shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room: Designed for infrastructure-heavy workflows, this level-based architecture scales Hermes from a single agent to automated teams capable of VPS provisioning and security auditing. It leverages a task bus pattern to manage handoffs and specialized skill sets for system administration.
entries.linke-ai/hermes-agent-team: A local collaboration system that orchestrates Leader and Specialist agents using MCP and ACP protocols for tool handling. The platform integrates a real-time Web UI and Hermes Kanban to monitor the entire lifecycle of agent dialogues and task progress.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project deploys a massive cohort of 17 specialized agents dedicated to distinct phases like research, planning, and infrastructure. It enforces strict quality gates through mandatory reviewer and tester agents to ensure code integrity across customizable pipelines.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A meta-optimization framework that uses an automated outer-loop search to refine the harness code of Hermes agents. It tracks performance frontiers by orchestrating TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, utilizing candidate mutation to improve agent efficiency.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes and Paperclip, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine agents per side and visualizes their code execution and scoreboard metrics on a real-time dashboard.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation tool routes tasks between Hermes, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution through isolated workflows. It prioritizes safety by enforcing configurable timeouts and strict output character limits on all delegated sub-tasks.

entries.colbymchenry/codegraph: This local indexing tool optimizes Hermes Agent performance by mapping code symbols into a knowledge graph, reducing tool calls by approximately 58%. It operates entirely without external dependencies, allowing agents to navigate complex repositories while significantly lowering token consumption.
entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A high-performance Rust TUI designed to track and visualize token usage across multiple AI coding clients, including Hermes and Claude Code. It aggregates data from local databases and JSONL files to provide developers with clear cost and usage insights.
entries.liaohch3/claude-tap: This local trace viewer enables developers to inspect API traffic, prompts, and tool calls generated by Hermes Agent and other coding assistants. It reconstructs streaming responses for debugging and can generate portable HTML artifacts for sharing or archiving session traces.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: A customization utility for the Hermes terminal interface that allows users to modify banner colors, ASCII art, and UI labels. It supports a library of themed presets and partial YAML configurations that inherit from default settings for easy personalization.
entries.Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes: An opinionated workflow layer that orchestrates 23 autonomous skills for building, shipping, and monitoring applications. It automates high-level tasks like GitHub issue triage and security reviews while integrating directly with platforms like Vercel and Supabase.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, eliminating the need for OAuth or external processes. It includes specialized MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training within the native environment.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser built for autonomous agent control via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It features a human-in-the-loop interface for action rollbacks and approvals, alongside durable session management for structured agent checkpoints.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific folders with project-local memories and tools. It allows developers to seamlessly switch between global and scoped configurations to maintain context-aware custom instructions.
entries.adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman: This setup utilizes RTK and Caveman templating to achieve up to 99% token savings through optimized CLI workflows. It comes pre-configured with over 40 skills for finance and DevOps, featuring an automated installer for macOS and Linux.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static linter that uses deterministic structural detectors to scan agent configurations and system prompts for failure modes without making LLM calls. It integrates into CI/CD pipelines to provide HERM v1.1 scoring and fast, cost-free validation of agent logic.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: A Docker-based deployment stack that configures Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for a zero-cost local setup. It includes 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, automating the routing for free models and local GPU inference.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: A specialized migration utility that transfers agent personas, memories, and workspace files from OpenClaw to the Hermes Agent format. It maps complex model configurations to Hermes YAML and handles the secure transfer of local credentials.

entries.farion1231/cc-switch: A cross-platform desktop manager built on Tauri 2 that provides a unified interface for Hermes Agent alongside other major CLI agents like Claude Code and Gemini. It simplifies the lifecycle of multiple AI tools by offering a single, high-performance controller for Windows, macOS, and Linux.
entries.iOfficeAI/AionUi: This comprehensive open-source workspace serves as a unified GUI for over 13 CLI agents, featuring a built-in engine for web search and MCP tool integration. It distinguishes itself with automated document generation capabilities, allowing users to create editable PowerPoint, Word, and Excel files directly from agent outputs.
entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A lightweight web and mobile interface that maintains full feature parity with the Hermes CLI through a zero-build architecture using Python and vanilla JavaScript. Its three-panel layout provides a streamlined workspace featuring an integrated file browser and inline previews for a native-feeling web experience.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A dedicated desktop companion designed to simplify the installation and configuration of Hermes Agent backends across 16 different messaging gateways. It provides visual management tools for fine-tuning personas, memory, and skills, making it an ideal entry point for users who prefer a guided setup.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: This dashboard focuses on multi-platform deployment, offering unified configuration for eight major chat services including Telegram, Discord, and Slack. It features robust session management and a web terminal, allowing administrators to monitor usage analytics and manage remote backends from a single interface.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace that introduces 'Swarm Mode' for orchestrating multiple persistent, role-based autonomous agents simultaneously. It offers zero-fork compatibility with vanilla Hermes installations, providing a seamless transition between chat, terminal, and memory editing panels.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A specialized macOS workspace that connects to Hermes hosts via SSH to provide a native desktop experience with real-time terminal access. It organizes agent activity through native Kanban views and file browsers, catering specifically to users managing remote agent instances.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted control plane that features the ZOO Swarm Monitor for tracking agent pipelines through a live event feed and Kanban board. It provides deep technical oversight with an MCP server configuration editor, log tailing, and real-time tool call cards.
entries.jazzyalex/agent-sessions: A native macOS session browser and 'Agent Cockpit' designed for live monitoring of active iTerm2 agent sessions. It prioritizes privacy with local-only indexing and no telemetry, allowing users to search and analyze conversation histories across multiple coding-agent CLIs.
entries.nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1: This macOS workspace is optimized for Orgo cloud computers, offering one-click automated installation of Hermes Agent on fresh cloud VMs. It stands out by integrating a WebRTC voice mode, enabling real-time vocal interaction with agents via OpenAI's Realtime API.
entries.liftaris/herm: An operator-focused TUI and dashboard built with OpenTUI that emphasizes rapid profile switching and session management. It includes a unique Kanban board for task dispatching and the Eikon Marketplace for discovering and installing shared agent extensions.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React dashboard designed to run as a Telegram Mini App, featuring secure HMAC-SHA256 authentication. It supports advanced mobile workflows including file attachments with automated vision analysis and OCR capabilities for on-the-go agent management.
entries.Eynzof/hermes-agent-cn-desktop: A Windows-first desktop application built with Tauri and Rust that provides a localized interface for the Chinese Hermes community. It streamlines the management of the Hermes kernel, offering integrated UI components for the skills library, memory management, and MCP tools.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install, portable Windows GUI that operates without admin rights or Docker, making it ideal for restricted environments. It comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools, including ComfyUI and MusicGen, and features a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A glassmorphic web interface that utilizes a single-file React architecture and a lightweight Python proxy for easy self-hosting. It provides real-time log streaming and a live workspace file browser to monitor agent activity and manage MCP tools in a modern UI.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-centric dashboard that provides a real-time activity feed with detailed cost tracking and model distribution analytics. It is designed for inspection and analysis, offering read-only access to skill sources and full-text search across all conversation histories.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that uses real-time SSE streaming and tool approval cards to give users granular control over agent actions. It features an integrated marketplace for MCP servers and isolated profile management to keep separate API keys and memories organized.
entries.Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop: A multi-agent desktop client that facilitates orchestrated collaboration between 20 specialized AI agents. It features a visual 'Skill Store' for one-click installations and a project management orchestrator to handle complex, multi-step agentic workflows.
